content.text: I'll read each frame and judge against the rubric.

[
{"ruleId":"V1","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"Each frame carries one focus: the prompt card alone, then the candidate/meter/score trio anchored by the growing arrow, then the centered walk-home question card.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V2","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"At most three or four elements appear together (card, meter, candidate arrow, score readout), well under the budget.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V3","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"'candidate' sits beside the arrow, 'meter' under the gauge, and 'score' is the readout itself; no boxed legend appears.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V4","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"On-screen text is confined to labels and short prompt cards ('retrieval prompt', 'for the walk home / same direction, smaller size?', '3 ways to continue').","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V5","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"All text including 'direction unchanged' and the score readout is comfortably readable at frame resolution.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V6","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"All cards, the meter, the candidate arrow, and readouts sit fully inside the frame with generous margins.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V7","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"No text or labels overlap; the arrow, score, meter, and cards occupy separate regions.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V8","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"Candidate/observed is periwinkle blue and the climbing score is lilac, consistent with token semantics across frames.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V9","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"No mascots or ornaments appear; the intervening blank frame is a transition rather than decorative filler.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V10","pass":true,"severity":"blocking","evidence":"The prompt card holds while the meter idles, the periwinkle candidate stretches longer as its lilac score climbs to 4.80 with 'direction unchanged', and the walk-home card plus '3 ways to continue' chip close the beat.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V11","pass":true,"severity":"advisory","evidence":"The 'direction unchanged' note sits near the candidate arrow and the score sits under the meter it drives.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V12","pass":true,"severity":"advisory","evidence":"Across frames the candidate arrow lengthens and the score climbs as a continuous progression rather than a cut.","suggestion":""},
{"ruleId":"V13","pass":true,"severity":"advisory","evidence":"Elements are spaced with ample negative space and a clear left-to-right reading order.","suggestion":""}
]
